text: ‘Eyes’ of a fountain, in the sense of openings. The Hebrew words for ‘head’ and ‘eye’ are also used for springs of water.
The word in the Hebrew text here represented by ‘serpents’ is generally translated ‘jackals’ by modern commentators.
This paragraph is thought by some to have been accidentally misplaced: it has little apparent connexion with its present context.
vv. 25, 26. This appears to be the sense intended by a passage of considerable obscurity.
